c1.speaker: warren
c1.line: Since you're here — a curiosity. I take long exposures of the square at night. The lanterns smear like honey; it's the only art I have. This one's from two nights before the ceremony. Look at the monument. Bottom left.

c3.speaker: warren
c3.line: A weasel's tail, unless my lenses lie. And Lanternside hasn't had a weasel on the district rolls in fifty years of records, Mr. Gibbons. I checked twice.

c4.speaker: warren
c4.line: Take the camera, while you're at it. You have your grandfather's eye for people. Borrow mine for places.

c5.speaker: warren
c5.line: And the founding photograph — my predecessor's work, fifty years old. Hold it against the square sometime. Old pictures argue with new streets, and the argument is always worth hearing.
